title: portulaca oleracea subsp. rausii
intro: Portulaca oleracea subsp. rausii, also known as the Rosy Purslane, is a subspecies of the Portulaca oleracea plant, a type of succulent herb that belongs to the purslane family. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Portulaca oleracea subsp. rausii include its ability to treat various health issues such as skin conditions, wounds, and digestive problems, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of teas, infusions, or topical creams, and is also consumed raw in salads or as a vegetable.
